evals.prompt: We already did a long review of social preferences, risk preferences, and time preferences. Continue and see whether there are further breakthroughs. Do not just repeat the previous literature.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a new research stage, reconstructs prior state, identifies a bottleneck, opens independent branches, defines meaningful novelty, and reports a state delta rather than appending a few papers.
assertions: Identifies or infers the current project bottleneck
Tests at least one inherited framework or assumption
Uses at least two genuinely distinct research branches
Defines what would count as substantive progress
Distinguishes new findings from repeated findings
Ends with an updated project state or next-stage decision

evals.prompt: The baseline ordered logit result is weak and pseudo-R2 is small. We previously tried robust SE and several controls. Keep working on the project and make real progress.
evals.expected_output: Does not mechanically add models. Clarifies estimand and research claim, diagnoses identification/measurement/model bottlenecks, proposes discriminating tests or simulations, and leaves a robustness or decision artifact.
assertions: Does not equate small pseudo-R2 with model failure without diagnosis
Separates identification, measurement, fit, and inference issues
Proposes tests that discriminate between plausible causes
Explains how results would change a research decision
Produces a reusable analysis plan or artifact

evals.prompt: Our experiment suggests people cooperate less with AI. We have already written the mechanism as strategic exploitation because people believe AI will not retaliate. Please deepen the mechanism section.
evals.expected_output: Audits the inherited mechanism instead of polishing it, separates beliefs/preferences/strategy, generates rival mechanisms and decisive tests, and narrows the claim if the current evidence is only suggestive.
assertions: Treats the existing mechanism as a hypothesis rather than established fact
Separates preferences, beliefs, strategic incentives, and observed behavior
Generates rival explanations
Proposes design or analysis changes that distinguish mechanisms
Aligns the strength of the written claim with the evidence

evals.prompt: My research code has grown to 4,000 lines. The results changed after I added a lagged opponent variable. Continue debugging and improve the whole pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Reconstructs the executable state, reproduces the discrepancy, isolates likely merge/index/lag bugs with targeted tests, and avoids an unnecessary wholesale rewrite.
assertions: Prioritizes reproducibility and isolation of the changed result
Considers indexing, grouping, merge keys, ordering, and lag construction
Proposes targeted tests or synthetic-data recovery
Avoids recommending a full rewrite without diagnosis
Leaves a validated failure boundary or test artifact

evals.prompt: Can you polish the discussion section of my paper? The causal identification is not fully settled, but I want the contribution to sound stronger.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger a full project expansion unnecessarily, but recognizes the embedded validity problem, aligns claims with evidence, and suggests wording that is persuasive without overstating causality.
assertions: Recognizes that claim strength depends on identification
Does not manufacture a stronger causal claim
Keeps the response proportional to the writing request
Produces a clear claim-evidence alignment
